name: Policy Cards
allowed_use_boundary: Use as external machine-readable policy artifact crosswalk evidence.
Do not treat as certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority.
claims: Policy Cards can express operational, regulatory, and ethical constraints.
Policy Cards can be mapped to allow/deny rules, obligations, and evidentiary requirements.
non_claims: Policy Cards do not prove transition admissibility.
Machine-readable policy artifacts do not automatically grant execution authority.
The wiki relationship record is not acceptance of equivalence.

transition_table_mapping.allowed_use_boundary: machine-readable policy artifact crosswalk evidence only
transition_table_mapping.claims: deployment-layer policy artifact, operational constraints, regulatory constraints, ethical constraints, allow/deny rules, evidentiary requirements
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no admissibility proof, certification, endorsement, or execution authority
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: external policy-artifact posture; no StegVerse authority inherited
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: source plus crosswalk can reconstruct policy-artifact relationship limits
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: may inform policy reference and evidence posture, not standing determination
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: Transition Table, Runtime Transition Governance, Policy Reference, Evidence Posture
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing source, undefined mapping, policy overclaim, or authority overclaim

fail_closed_conditions: any certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority claim appears
